steps: 1. Chop potato. 2. Heat oil. 3. Add spices. 4. Add potato. 5. Cook. 6. Serve. 7. Dry. 8. Snack.

1. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large pan over medium heat. 2. Add 1 teaspoon of cumin seeds and let them splutter. 3. Add 1 finely chopped onion and sauté until golden brown. 4. Add 2 chopped tomatoes and cook until they turn mushy. 5. Add 2 potatoes and 1 cauliflower, cut into pieces, along with spices like turmeric, chili, and garam masala. 6. Stir well to coat the vegetables. 7. Cover and cook for 20-25 minutes until tender. 8. Garnish with coriander and serve with roti.
1. Chop potato. 2. Heat oil. 3. Add spices. 4. Add veggies. 5. Cook. 6. Serve. 7. With puri. 8. Simple.

1. Boil 2 potatoes and mash them. 2. Chop 1 onion and mix with potatoes. 3. Add spices like chili and coriander. 4. Knead dough with 1 cup flour and water. 5. Divide dough and stuff with potato mix. 6. Roll into parathas. 7. Cook on hot griddle with oil. 8. Serve with yogurt.

1. Roast 1 large eggplant over flame until charred. 2. Peel and mash the eggplant. 3. Chop 1 onion and 1 tomato. 4. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. 5. Sauté onion until golden. 6. Add tomato and spices, cook until soft. 7. Mix in mashed eggplant. 8. Cook for 5 minutes and serve with bread.

1. Mix 2 cups puffed rice in a bowl. 2. Chop potatoes, onions, and tomatoes. 3. Add to rice with chutneys. 4. Sprinkle sev and peanuts. 5. Add lemon juice and spices .6 Toss well .7 Garnish with coriander .8 Serve immediately.

1. Marinate chicken in yogurt and spices. 2. Let it rest for 2 hours. 3. Preheat oven. 4. Place on baking tray. 5. Bake at 200°C for 25 minutes. 6. Turn halfway. 7. Brush with oil. 8. Serve with salad.

1. Heat oil in a pan. 2. Roast 1 cup flattened rice. 3. Add 1/2 cup peanuts, roast. 4. Add spices like chili. 5. Mix well. 6. Remove from heat. 7. Cool completely. 8. Store in airtight container.
1. Wash and slice 1 cucumber. 2. Chop 1 tomato and 1 onion. 3. Place in a bowl. 4. Squeeze juice from 1 lemon. 5. Drizzle over vegetables. 6. Season with salt. 7. Toss well. 8. Chill and serve.

1. Soak 1 cup urad dal and grind into batter. 2. Shape into balls and deep fry until golden. 3. Soak in water for 10 minutes, then squeeze. 4. Mix with sweetened yogurt. 5. Add spices and tamarind chutney. 6. Chill for 30 minutes. 7. Garnish with nuts. 8. Serve cold.

1. Grind soaked lentils into batter. 2. Shape into vadas and fry. 3. Soak in water, then squeeze. 4. Dip in yogurt. 5. Add spices and chutney. 6. Chill for 1 hour. 7. Garnish with nuts. 8. Serve cold.

1. Rinse 1 cup toor dal. 2. Pressure cook with water for 10 minutes. 3. Heat 1 tablespoon ghee. 4. Add cumin seeds and red chili. 5. Add hing and curry leaves. 6. Pour over dal. 7. Boil for 5 minutes. 8. Serve with rice.

1. Boil eggs and fry. 2. Cook rice with spices. 3. Sauté onions. 4. Layer rice and eggs. 5. Add saffron. 6. Cook covered. 7. Let sit. 8. Serve warm.

1. Beat 2 eggs in a bowl. 2. Chop 1 onion and 1 green chili finely. 3. Add onion and chili to the eggs. 4. Season with salt and pepper. 5. Heat 1 teaspoon oil in a pan. 6. Pour the egg mixture into the pan. 7. Cook for 2-3 minutes until set. 8. Fold and serve hot.

1. Clean and cut 200g fish into pieces. 2. Chop 1 onion and 1 tomato finely. 3. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. 4. Add chopped onion and sauté until brown. 5. Garnish with coriander and serve with rice.
1. Clean and cut fish. 2. Marinate in spices. 3. Sauté onions. 4. Add tomatoes and spices. 5. Cook masala. 6. Add fish and simmer. 7. Cook until fish is done. 8. Serve with rice.
1. Cut fish into cubes. 2. Marinate in yogurt and spices. 3. Skewer the pieces. 4. Preheat grill. 5. Grill for 10 minutes. 6. Turn occasionally. 7. Brush with oil. 8. Serve with lemon.

1. Wash all the fruits thoroughly under running water. 2. Peel the apple, banana, and papaya if necessary. 3. Chop the apple, banana, papaya, and grapes into bite-sized pieces. 4. Place all the chopped fruits in a large mixing bowl. 5. Gently toss the fruits together to mix evenly. 6. Drizzle with a squeeze of lemon juice to prevent browning (optional). 7. Chill the salad in the refrigerator for 15-20 minutes. 8. Serve fresh as a refreshing snack.

Add chopped onion and sauté until golden. 6. Add carrot and peas, cook for 5 minutes. 7. Drain poha and add to the pan, mix well. 8. Cook for 2-3 minutes and serve.

1. Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a pan over medium heat. 2. Add 1/2 teaspoon of mustard seeds and allow them to splutter. 3. Add 1 finely chopped onion and sauté until it turns golden brown. 4. Add 1 chopped carrot and 1/2 cup chopped beans, and cook for 5 minutes until slightly tender. 5. Stir in 1 cup of roasted semolina (rava) and mix well to combine. 6. Gradually pour in 2 cups of water while stirring continuously to avoid lumps. 7. Cook the mixture on low heat for 10-15 minutes until the water is absorbed and the upma thickens. 8. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot.
